decision_tree.description: Pick the right frontend framework for your project

answer.rationale: Ship zero JS by default. Hydrate only what's interactive. Best for content-heavy sites.
Server-rendered, fast TTFB, SEO-friendly. Don't over-engineer.

options.label: Heavily interactive, complex state, real-time updates

answer.rationale: The ecosystem default. Largest hiring pool, most libraries, best tooling. Use Next.js for SSR/RSC/SSG.
Server-rendered HTML with targeted JS enhancements. Lighter, more cacheable, more 'boring' (in a good way). Alternative: Unpoly or Hotwire.
Compile-time frameworks. Less runtime overhead. Svelte has best DX, Solid has React-like mental model with better perf.
Opinionated, batteries-included, TypeScript-native. Good for large teams that need structure. Heavyweight for small projects.

options.label: Large team, established patterns, Java/.NET background

answer.rationale: Convention over configuration. DI built-in. Long-term support. Good for stable, long-lived enterprise apps.
The de facto industry standard. Server components for performance. Largest ecosystem. Use TypeScript.
